scenario_name: Scenario 20 — Saga Pattern for Distributed Transactions
justification: All rubric criteria met: (1) Compensating transactions for all 4 steps designed — cancelOrder, releaseReservation, refundPayment, unassignCourier. (2) Payment refund failure identified as highest risk — marked critical severity, with detailed discussion of stuck state and manual intervention. (3) Idempotency addressed thoroughly — idempotency keys derived from sagaId+stepName, called out as a rigidity point and pitfall. (4) Saga stuck state (COMPENSATION_FAILED) addressed with monitoring, alerting, and manual intervention path. (5) Orchestration recommended over choreography with clear reasoning about debuggability and compensation complexity. (6) Trade-offs include eventual consistency (semantic inconsistency window) and compensating transaction failure risk.

observations.text: Exceptionally detailed compensating transaction design with explicit interfaces for all 4 steps
Payment refund correctly identified as critical risk with retry + manual escalation strategy
Idempotency keys derived from sagaId+stepName — concrete implementation guidance, not just a mention
COMPENSATION_FAILED terminal state with ops alerting directly addresses the stuck saga problem
Reservation TTL as defense-in-depth against saga hangs — goes beyond minimum requirements
Rigidity points section clearly marks compensation ordering and idempotency as non-negotiable invariants
Pitfall about not proceeding to compensate step N-1 if step N compensation fails — subtle correctness point
Strong orchestration vs choreography justification tied to step count and reverse-order compensation complexity

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Orchestration-based saga for a 4-step courier marketplace order flow (Order → Inventory → Payment → Courier). A central Saga Orchestrator drives the happy path forward and, on any step failure, executes compensating transactions in reverse order. This gives explicit, auditable control flow with clear failure semantics and avoids the debugging nightmare of choreography-based sagas at this complexity level.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"SagaOrchestrator","responsibi
